slides.title: The Digital World and Future Innovation
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 9: The Digital World and Future Innovation. Welcome to Unit 9: The Digital World and Future Innovation — the final unit of Year 7! In this unit you will explore how digital technology, robotics, and innovative farming are reshaping daily life across East Africa and the wider world. You will read about young people who use smartphones, coding skills, and creative thinking to solve real problems in their communities. Through reading, writing, speaking, and grammar activities, you will sharpen your English skills while thinking critically about the role of technology in our lives. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand a passage about digital innovation and discuss its ideas confidently in English
Use reported speech to relay what someone has said: “She said that the app had just been updated.”
Form conditional sentences with if and unless: “If we use technology wisely, it will help us.”
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand a passage about digital innovation and discuss its ideas confidently in english. you will also use reported speech to relay what someone has said: “she said that the app had just been updated.”. you will also form conditional sentences with if and unless: “if we use technology wisely, it will help us.”. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: agitate, conceive, converge, introduce, reveal, recognise. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: The Digital World and Future Innovation: source text 1
The Digital World and Future Innovation: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Digital World and Future Innovation: source text 1 and The Digital World and Future Innovation: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Perfect + Just
bullets: What it means and why it matters The present perfect tense is used to talk about actions that have happened at an unspecified time before now. When we add...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Perfect + Just. What it means and why it matters The present perfect tense is used to talk about actions that have happened at an unspecified time before now. When we add just, it means the action happened very recently. This is important in the digital world because technology changes fast — we often need to share updates and news that feel immediate and fresh. For example, if you say, “She has just uploaded the code,” it tells us the upload happened only a moment ago. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
